goal: Help developers understand and trust autonomous code changes in this repository.

memory.goal: Differentiate Waywright from generic autonomous coding agents
memory.selectedDirection: Shared engineering memory with a cognitive-debt UI
memory.rationale: The agent and humans should use the same record of decisions, rejected alternatives, and real execution outcomes. The agent uses it to choose what to build next; humans use it to reconstruct why.

rejected.reason: The category is crowded and direction execution alone is not differentiated.
A final summary hides rejected alternatives and does not carry lessons into the next decision.

evidence: Buildkite MCP already created and observed a real build, proving outcomes can be stored alongside decisions.
The product ideation identified decision amnesia for agents and cognitive debt for humans as two sides of the same missing memory.

outcome.summary: Selected for implementation during the hackathon.

lessons: A remembered decision must include why, what was rejected, and what actually happened.
Human understanding is one output edge; memory-grounded direction finding remains the core behavior.

context.summary: demo-target — goal: Help developers understand and trust autonomous code changes in this repository.

context.evidence:
# demo-target

To install dependencies:

```bash
bun install
```

To run:

```bash
bun run index.ts
```

This project was created using `bun init` in bun v1.3.14. [Bun](https://bun.com) is a fast all-in-one JavaScript runtime.

candidates.title: Render decision-linked diff annotations in PR/commit view
candidates.hypothesis: Surfacing each hunk's originating decision (why, what was rejected, what happened) inline in the diff reduces reviewer time-to-trust more than a plain PR summary
acceptanceCriteria: Diff view renders a rationale marker per changed hunk pointing to its memory record
Marker shows selected direction + at least one rejected alternative + outcome status
Manual test: reviewer can answer 'why was X changed' without leaving the diff for 3 sample PRs

candidates.title: CLI/script to query 'why' for any file or line from stored decision records
candidates.hypothesis: A queryable command (e.g. `why <file>:<line>`) grounded in typed decision records is more useful for trust-building than passive documentation since it works on-demand during code review, not just at merge time
acceptanceCriteria: Command resolves a file/line or commit SHA to its decision record(s) via git blame + memory lookup
Output includes rationale, rejected alternatives, and evidence fields verbatim (no LLM restatement, per D-000/4151)
Works for at least the last 5 real commits in this repo as a smoke test

candidates.title: Human-readable explanation of merge-gate pass/fail tied to Pomerium policy decisions
candidates.hypothesis: Since merge safety already relies on a fail-closed Pomerium boundary (per memory), exposing *why* a specific autonomous change passed or was blocked builds trust at the highest-stakes moment (merge time) more directly than general diff annotation
acceptanceCriteria: Each merge attempt logs which policy rule allowed/blocked it plus the decision record that motivated that policy
Blocked merges show a human-readable reason distinct from raw Pomerium/HTTP status
Verified against at least one real allowed and one real blocked merge in this repo's history

priorArt.decision: Preflight repository trust before adopting its design patterns
priorArt.why: Zero.xyz capability run run_mynW_IFwfir4BwDBgxX4J: {"repo":"argotorg/sourcify","url":"https://github.com/argotorg/sourcify","description":"Source code verification service for Ethereum smart contracts","homepage":"https://sourcify.dev","stars":941,"forks":537,"open_issues":105,"watchers":941,"primary_language":"TypeScript","languages":{"TypeScript":1692194,"Solidity":1098964,"JavaScript":140831,"PLpgSQL":65159,"Shell":32055,"Vyper":6362,"Dockerfile":3932,"Yul":1622},"license":"MIT License","license_spdx":"MIT","topics":["ethereum","smart-contracts","solidity","source-code-verification","verification","vyper"],"created_at":"2019-04-24T08:23:22Z

priorArt.decision: Every match result carries a typed status enum (perfect/partial/null) plus a structured `message` and a `transformations`/`transformationValues` list explaining exactly which bytecode differences (constructor args, library links, immutables, metadata hash) were normalized away to reach the match.
priorArt.why: For decision-linked diff annotations, each hunk needs a machine-readable 'why this was accepted/rejected' payload, not prose — Sourcify's transformation list is a ready template for 'what was normalized/rejected and why' per code region.

priorArt.decision: Verification failures are modeled as a closed set of typed error codes (VerificationErrorCode: contract_not_deployed, bytecode_length_mismatch, no_match, etc.) thrown via a single VerificationError class, rather than free-text exceptions.
priorArt.why: A closed enum of rejection reasons is exactly what inline diff annotations need to render consistent, filterable badges ('rejected: bytecode_length_mismatch') instead of parsing arbitrary error strings.

priorArt.decision: The core library (packages/lib-sourcify) is fully decoupled from the API/server layer — verification produces a structured result object first, and only the server layer decides how to surface it (DB rows, API fields, docs) via an explicit FIELDS_TO_STORED_PROPERTIES mapping that must stay in sync with the OpenAPI spec.
priorArt.why: Keeping the 'decision record' (structured result) separate from its presentation lets the same verification/decision data feed multiple views (API response, PR annotation, DB) without re-deriving 'why' at render time — mirrors the separation needed between a decision-recording step and a diff-rendering step.

priorArt.decision: Preflight repository trust before adopting its design patterns
priorArt.why: Zero.xyz capability run run_u4VtwIUf_pfYvUR07Zhav: {"repo":"open-policy-agent/opa","url":"https://github.com/open-policy-agent/opa","description":"Open Policy Agent (OPA) is an open source, general-purpose policy engine.","homepage":"https://www.openpolicyagent.org","stars":11991,"forks":1617,"open_issues":348,"watchers":11991,"primary_language":"Go","languages":{"Go":9872592,"C":944882,"C++":710174,"Open Policy Agent":117885,"Makefile":22951,"Shell":22171,"JavaScript":15600,"Python":5616,"Dockerfile":2622,"TypeScript":243},"license":"Apache License 2.0","license_spdx":"Apache-2.0","topics":["authorization","cloud-native","compliance","declara

priorArt.decision: Structured Event trace with typed Op enum (Enter/Eval/Fail/Exit/Note/...) plus a separate PrettyTrace renderer that formats the same events for humans
priorArt.why: OPA separates the machine-readable decision trace (Event structs carrying Node, Location, QueryID, ParentID) from its human rendering. For merge-gate pass/fail explanations, emit a typed decision-trace (which policy rule fired, which condition failed) as structured data first, then render it — so the same trace can back both a CLI message and a machine-checkable audit log.

priorArt.decision: Signed decision payload via a pluggable Signer interface producing a JWT over the exact file/decision set, with keyID and plugin name embedded
priorArt.why: Pomerium's fail-closed gate is only trustworthy if the pass/fail explanation can't be forged or replayed against a different diff. Steal the pattern of binding an explanation to a signed, keyed token over the specific inputs it covers (not just returning a boolean), so the explanation itself is verifiable evidence, not just log text.

priorArt.decision: FailOp/NoteOp as first-class trace events distinct from generic Eval — failure and explicit annotation get their own operation types rather than being inferred post-hoc from a generic log line
priorArt.why: Root-causing *why* a merge was blocked requires the failure point to be explicit at emission time, not reconstructed by diffing eval logs. Emitting a dedicated 'blocked-by' event (analogous to FailOp) at the exact Pomerium policy clause that denied the change makes the merge-gate explanation deterministic and directly traceable to one rule.

decision.rationale: D-000 established that shared engineering memory (decisions + rejections + outcomes) is the differentiator, and its explicit consumer edge is 'humans use it to reconstruct why.' annotated-diff-rationale is the direct implementation of that human-facing edge: it surfaces the decision record at the exact point (diff/PR view) where a developer is trying to trust an autonomous change. Sourcify's prior art gives a concrete, already-proven template — typed status enum + structured transformation list + closed-set error codes + decoupled result-object-from-presentation — for exactly the payload this direction needs per hunk, and their MIT-licensed, actively maintained repo (941 stars, TS-heavy) is a legitimate pattern source. This is a natural evolution of D-000's rationale, not a reversal.

rejected.reason: An on-demand CLI is a weaker default UX for building trust than diff annotations: it requires the developer to already suspect something and issue a manual query, whereas the goal is fluent, unavoidable exposure of rationale during code review. No prior-art evidence was supplied for this candidate, unlike the other two, indicating shallower validation.

Targets a narrower, already-covered edge (per memory 4148, the Pomerium fail-closed boundary is established) — it explains policy pass/fail, not general code trust. It's a good future direction but is downstream of and dependent on decision records already being rendered legibly, which is exactly what annotated-diff-rationale builds first. Its prior art (OPA signed traces) is also heavier infrastructure (pluggable Signer/JWT) than the hackathon scope likely affords.

evidence: memory D-000: 'humans use it to reconstruct why' names diff/PR-time explanation as the human consumer edge of shared engineering memory
priorArt ethereum/sourcify: transformations/transformationValues list is a ready template for per-hunk 'what was normalized/rejected and why' payloads
priorArt ethereum/sourcify: closed VerificationErrorCode enum enables consistent, filterable 'rejected: X' badges instead of free-text parsing
priorArt ethereum/sourcify: lib-sourcify/server decoupling shows structured decision objects can feed multiple views (API, PR annotation, DB) without re-deriving rationale at render time
context repository: demo-target has no existing scripts/pipeline, so a diff-rendering feature is greenfield and low-conflict to add now

selected.title: Render decision-linked diff annotations in PR/commit view
selected.hypothesis: Surfacing each hunk's originating decision (why, what was rejected, what happened) inline in the diff reduces reviewer time-to-trust more than a plain PR summary
acceptanceCriteria: Diff view renders a rationale marker per changed hunk pointing to its memory record
Marker shows selected direction + at least one rejected alternative + outcome status
Manual test: reviewer can answer 'why was X changed' without leaving the diff for 3 sample PRs

plan:
```markdown
# Plan: Decision-Linked Diff Annotations (annotated-diff-rationale)

## Scope

Attach the run's decision record (selected direction, rejection reasons, evidence, outcome) to every changed hunk in the actuator's diff, and render it inline in `report.html` next to the diff — not just in the existing "MEMORY WRITTEN" drawer, which today summarizes the whole run but never touches the actual code.

Ouroboros currently produces one `DirectionDecision` per run (`src/types.ts:39`), not one per hunk — the actuator (`src/actuator.ts`) implements a single plan on a single branch. This plan does **not** invent per-hunk sub-decisions; every hunk in a given run gets the same decision payload. If a future run starts splitting a plan into independently-decided steps, per-hunk decisions become a real need — not now.

## Files

- `src/diff.ts` **(new)** — parse `git diff` output into hunks (`{ file, startLine, header, lines }`). Pure function, no git calls itself.
- `src/actuator.ts` — after `deps.implement(plan)` returns a branch, capture `git diff <base>...<branch>` via a new `ActuatorDeps.diff(branch): Promise<string>` dependency; thread the raw diff text into `ActuatorResult` as `diff: string`.
- `src/types.ts` — add `diff?: string` to `ActuatorResult`.
- `src/render-report.ts` — add `renderDiffAnnotations(actuation, decision, memory)`: for each parsed hunk, render a marker showing selected direction title, first rejected alternative + reason, and outcome status, linking to the matching memory record (reuse existing `memoryMatches`/highlight wiring already built for evidence rows).
- `src/report.ts` — pass `actuation?.diff` through to the render call (already assembles `WaywrightReportModel` inputs from `.waywright/*.json`; no new artifact file needed since diff now lives in `actuation.json`).
- `tests/diff.test.ts` **(new)** — RED/GREEN for the parser.
- `tests/render-report.test.ts` — extend existing suite (or add if absent) for the new annotation block.
- `tests/actuator.test.ts` — extend for the new `diff` dependency call and result field.

## Non-goals

- No per-hunk distinct decisions/sub-rationale — out of scope until the actuator itself plans in multiple independently-decided steps.
- No new closed-set error/status enum modeled on Sourcify's `VerificationErrorCode` — Ouroboros already has a closed `outcome.status` enum (`passed | failed | denied | unknown` in `types.ts:17`); reuse it, don't add a parallel one.
- No GitHub PR inline-comment posting (i.e., not writing annotations onto the actual GitHub diff via API) — this plan renders inside the existing self-hosted `report.html`, which is the artifact the demo already drives users to. Posting real PR review comments is a separate, higher-privilege integration and not needed to hit the acceptance criteria.
- No new storage layer — decision data already lives in `NavigationResult`/`EngineeringMemoryRecord` (`src/types.ts`) and is already piped into `report.ts`; this plan only adds a diff string and a rendering function, reusing `EngineeringMemory`/gbrain as-is.
- No diff-viewer library (no `diff2html`, no Monaco) — the existing hunk header + line list renders fine as plain HTML given the report's current dark-mode design system; adding a dependency for this is against the ladder (rung 5 fails, rung 7 — minimum code — wins).

## RED tests (write first, must fail)

1. `tests/diff.test.ts`
   - `parseDiff` on a two-file unified diff returns hunks with correct `file`, `startLine`, and `lines` for each file — fails because `src/diff.ts` doesn't exist.
   - `parseDiff("")` returns `[]` (empty diff → no hunks, not a crash).
2. `tests/actuator.test.ts`
   - `actuate()` calls `deps.diff(branch)` exactly once after a successful build and stores the result on `ActuatorResult.diff` — fails because `ActuatorDeps` has no `diff` method and `actuate` never calls it.
3. `tests/render-report.test.ts` (new or extended)
   - Given a navigation with one candidate decision and an actuation with a 1-hunk diff, `renderWaywrightReport` output contains a rationale marker whose text includes the selected direction's title, the first rejected reason, and the outcome status string — fails because no such markup exists yet.
   - Given `actuation.diff` is `undefined` (build not yet run), the report still renders without throwing and shows an empty-state message for diff annotations (mirrors existing `empty()` pattern) — fails because the new render function doesn't exist.

## GREEN implementation

- `src/diff.ts`: minimal unified-diff parser — split on `diff --git`/`@@` markers, no external diff library (rung 3/5 fail: no diff-parsing dep is already installed and stdlib doesn't parse unified diff format, so this is the one place custom code is justified — but keep it to string splitting, not a full patch AST).
- `src/actuator.ts`: add `diff(branch: string): Promise<string>` to `ActuatorDeps`, call once right after the green build is confirmed (not per iteration — only the final merged/denied state needs annotating), store on result.
- `src/render-report.ts`: `renderDiffAnnotations` reuses `escapeHtml`, `memoryMatches`, and the existing `.evidence-copy`/`data-memory-ids` click-to-highlight wiring so the new markers participate in the existing memory-highlight interaction instead of building a parallel one.
- Wire the new section into the `written-grid`/execution area of the existing template in `render-report.ts`, keeping the current CSS variables/classes rather than introducing new design tokens.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `report.html` renders one rationale marker per changed hunk (RED test 3, part 1).
- [ ] Each marker shows: selected direction title, ≥1 rejected alternative + its reason, and outcome status (RED test 3, part 1).
- [ ] Manual check: for 3 sample runs (`.waywright/navigation.json` + `actuation.json` fixtures, reuse or extend fixtures already in `tests/report.test.ts`), a reviewer can state why a specific hunk changed by reading the annotation alone, without opening `.waywright/navigation.json` directly.
- [ ] No regression: report still renders when `actuation.diff` is absent (pre-build state) or `actuation` itself is `undefined` (nav-only state), matching current `renderExecution`'s pending-state handling.
- [ ] `bun test` green across `diff.test.ts`, `actuator.test.ts`, `render-report`/`report.test.ts`.

## Adopted prior-art decisions (from DECISION.evidence)

- Sourcify's per-result `transformations`/structured `message` list → adopted as the *shape* of the per-hunk payload (direction title + rejected reason + status), not as new code — Ouroboros's `EngineeringMemoryRecord` (`types.ts:8`) already carries this shape, so no new type is introduced, only a rendering path.
- Sourcify's decoupling of the structured result object from its presentation layer (`lib-sourcify` vs. server `FIELDS_TO_STORED_PROPERTIES`) → adopted by keeping `diff.ts` (parsing) and `render-report.ts` (presentation) as separate modules, so the same decision data can later feed a different view (e.g. a future PR-comment poster) without re-deriving rationale.
- Sourcify's closed `VerificationErrorCode` enum → explicitly **not** re-implemented; Ouroboros's existing `outcome.status` enum already satisfies the "consistent, filterable badge" need this pattern was cited for, per the non-goals above.
```
